title: ADP (ADP) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on ADP using FY2024 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what ADP is really worth. Economic Prospect Score: 82/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-77x5fwy2>
ADP is a mature compounder. An 8% rate accounts for consistent high single-digit revenue growth and modest margin expansion.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-77x5fwy2>
10Y Treasury: 4.18%. For a defensive company with high recurring revenue, a 9% discount rate provides a solid required return.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-77x5fwy2>
Long-term nominal GDP growth is ~3%. ADP operates as a tax on employment; they will grow steadily alongside the overall economy.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use DCF instead of a P/E multiple?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "ADP (ADP) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, 2026-03-19.

prospectVerdictDetail: Automatic Data Processing (ADP) maintains a wide economic moat built on exceptionally high switching costs within its core payroll and human capital management (HCM) platforms. As the dominant player in the industry, processing payroll for one in six U.S. workers, its network effects and scale advantages are formidable. While top-line growth is generally steady rather than explosive, expanding operating margins and a massive float on client funds—which benefits from higher interest rates—provide a robust foundation. The recent financial performance, with FY2025 revenue nearing $20.5 billion, underscores its consistent cash generation.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: ADP's competitive momentum is driven by its vast scale and expanding suite of HCM solutions, though growth is steady rather than rapid compared to purely cloud-native disruptors.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: With nearly $20.5 billion in FY25 revenue, ADP's growth is predictable and solid, often in the mid-to-high single digits. While it may lag the hyper-growth of smaller cloud-native peers like Workday in certain segments, its massive base provides immense stability.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: ADP continues to hold a dominant market share in the U.S. payroll processing space. Its upmarket push and international expansion help defend against encroachment from smaller, specialized players.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: The mission-critical nature of payroll and HR software grants ADP significant pricing power. Price increases are consistently realized with minimal client attrition due to the high disruption associated with changing providers.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: While ADP continues to modernize its tech stack and integrate AI into its HCM suites (like ADP Lyric), its sheer size can make product velocity slower compared to nimble, pure-SaaS competitors.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: The durability of ADP's moat is exceptionally high, rooted in steep switching costs and significant scale advantages that deter new entrants.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Switching payroll and core HR systems is incredibly disruptive, risky, and time-consuming for any organization. This creates immense lock-in and structural stickiness for ADP's client base.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: While not a traditional two-sided network, ADP benefits from massive scale and data advantages. Processing payroll for millions provides unique labor market insights that are increasingly monetized and used to enhance product offerings.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The complex and ever-changing nature of tax codes, compliance, and employment law acts as a significant barrier to entry, heavily favoring established incumbents with vast regulatory expertise like ADP.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: ADP operates a highly capital-efficient, scalable software and services model. Additionally, it benefits uniquely from 'float'—earning interest on client funds held before payroll is disbursed, requiring virtually no additional capital investment.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment remains positive due to ADP's defensive characteristics and reliable capital returns, though macroeconomic labor trends remain a key variable.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts maintain steady positive revisions, recognizing the defensive nature of the business model and the structural boost to EPS from the sustained higher interest rate environment impacting float income.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is stable, framing ADP as a blue-chip 'Dividend King' that provides a safe harbor during economic uncertainty, though it lacks the explosive AI narrative of tech giants.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management executes a shareholder-friendly capital allocation strategy, highlighted by decades of consecutive dividend increases and consistent share repurchases funded by strong free cash flow.

prospectKeyRisks: A severe macroeconomic downturn leading to significantly higher unemployment would directly impact ADP's 'pays per control' metrics and overall revenue.
Intensifying competition from modern, cloud-native HCM platforms (e.g., Workday, Paycom) moving down-market or disrupting specific product verticals.
A sudden and sharp decline in interest rates would materially reduce the high-margin interest income earned on client fund balances (float).
prospectKeyCatalysts: Continued US employment strength and wage inflation drives pays-per-control growth and higher payroll processing values, providing an organic revenue tailwind without new client acquisition
HCM platform expansion into benefits administration, workforce analytics, and compliance-as-a-service increases revenue per client by 30-50%, deepening the relationship moat

International expansion, particularly in Europe where payroll complexity is even greater and fragmentation higher, could add 2-3 points of incremental growth as multinational clients consolidate providers
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored ADP at 82/100 and Opus at 80/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
